appName: Emergency Gear Inventory and Maintenance Tracker

kits.name: Dad Go-Bag
kits.description: Priority 1 personal backpack for immediate evacuation.

kits.name: Mom Go-Bag
kits.description: Priority 1 personal backpack plus family medical information.

kits.name: Clara Go-Bag
kits.description: Priority 1 child comfort and clothing backpack.

kits.name: Grant Go-Bag
kits.description: Priority 1 child comfort, clothing, and activity backpack.

kits.name: Elise Go-Bag
kits.description: Priority 1 small child comfort and clothing backpack.

kits.name: Family Medical Bag
kits.description: Shared first aid, trauma, medicine, and electrolyte supplies.

kits.name: Truck Kit
kits.description: Priority 2 vehicle support, water, food, tools, and storm cleanup gear.

kits.name: Trailer Kit
kits.description: Priority 3 extended water, food, power, shelter, and tool reserve if safe and time permits.

kits.name: Last-Minute Loading List
kits.description: Items not stored in bags but worth loading when there is warning time.

kits.name: Departure Checklist
kits.description: Final household and travel checks before evacuation.

items.itemName: House Water Turned Off if Needed

skills.skill: Place generator outdoors away from doors, windows, and vents
Keep generator dry and protected from rain without enclosing it
Manage generator load and avoid overload

challenges.title: First aid night
challenges.focus: Practice simple household first aid and medical bag familiarity.

challenges.title: Knot tying
challenges.focus: Practice square knot, bowline, coiling rope, and paracord uses.

challenges.title: Emergency communications
challenges.focus: Practice phone numbers, weather radio, whistle, and radios.

challenges.title: Shelter building
challenges.focus: Practice tarp shelter basics and rain gear checks.

challenges.title: Water purification
challenges.focus: Practice filters, tablets, water rotation, and safe filling.

challenges.title: Camp cooking
challenges.focus: Practice camp stove safety and simple meals.

challenges.title: Hurricane preparation
challenges.focus: Review bags, truck kit, trailer kit, and evacuation order.

challenges.title: Navigation
challenges.focus: Practice maps, compass, and family meeting points.

challenges.title: Home emergency drill
challenges.focus: Run a calm home readiness and departure checklist drill.

challenges.title: Fire safety
challenges.focus: Practice fire safety, extinguishing, and safe tool boundaries.

challenges.title: Power outage simulation
challenges.focus: Practice lights, battery banks, generator basics, carbon monoxide awareness, and radios.

challenges.title: Gear inspection and repacking
challenges.focus: Inspect, repack, rotate food/water, and export a backup.

maintenancePlans.name: 2014 Silverado
maintenancePlans.notes: Baseline family readiness plan. Confirm exact service intervals against the owner's manual and actual engine/drivetrain.

tasks.notes: Track mileage and use the owner's manual interval for the exact engine.
Check spare tire too, especially before evacuation travel.
Use mileage interval if preferred.
Clean corrosion and verify strong starts before storm season.
Pads, rotors, fluid level, and pedal feel.
Storm travel visibility check.
Seasonal under-hood inspection.
Exercise controls briefly in a safe location.

tasks.task: Trailer hitch, safety chains, and wiring
tasks.notes: Confirm receiver, ball mount, chains, and 7-pin/lighting before towing.
Keep above 3/4 tank when storms threaten.

maintenancePlans.name: 2015 Jayco Whitehawk 28DSBH
maintenancePlans.notes: Baseline trailer readiness plan. Confirm intervals against Jayco/component manuals, especially brakes, bearings, tires, propane, and roof sealants.

tasks.task: Trailer tire pressure, age, and sidewalls
tasks.notes: Check before every trip; include spare tire.
Follow axle manufacturer guidance; important before long evacuation towing.
Test breakaway switch and braking response safely.

tasks.task: 7-pin connector, running lights, brake lights, and signals
tasks.notes: Check before towing.

tasks.task: Roof, seams, windows, and sealant inspection
tasks.notes: Look for cracks, gaps, soft spots, or water intrusion.
Use qualified service for leaks or component issues.
Charge monthly and inspect terminals.
Seasonal water readiness.
Inspect, clean, and lubricate per manual.

tasks.task: Fire extinguisher, smoke alarm, and CO alarm
tasks.notes: Check dates, charge indicators, and test buttons.
